# Network Fixtures — Interface Status

Captured output for the **Network Adapter** (`bcs.platform.adapters.network`,
`ip`-backed, see [docs/NETWORK_ADAPTER.md](../../../../docs/NETWORK_ADAPTER.md)).
General collection, locale, anonymization, naming, and placeholder rules are in the
[corpus root README](../README.md) and are not restated here.

## Required Command Line

Exactly the invocation the adapter's accepted design specifies:

```
LC_ALL=C LANG=C ip -json addr show > <fixture-name>.txt
```

Record the tool version (`ip --version`, first line) in the inventory table
below and in the fixture's filename.

## Layout

No vendor subdirectories, unlike `firmware/`. `ip`'s JSON output is generated by
the kernel's netlink interface, which is not vendor-specific — a flat
`network/*.txt` layout mirrors `secureboot/`'s own precedent and rationale
exactly. All fixtures live directly under this directory.

> **Note on `ip` version:** The `ip` tool version (from `ip --version`) should
> be included in the filename. For placeholder files, `unknown` is used until a
> real capture exists.

## Inventory

Every fixture (including placeholders) gets a row here. **All current
entries are zero-byte placeholders** — no real output has been captured
yet; see the root README's placeholder rules.

| File | Scenario | Status | Tool version | Captured on / from | Anonymized |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `ip_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_ethernet-up.txt` | One Ethernet interface UP with an IPv4 address — the baseline case | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `ip_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_ethernet-down.txt` | Interface present but administratively DOWN (no `LOWER_UP` flag) | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `ip_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_multi-interface.txt` | Ethernet + loopback + WiFi — the common real-machine case | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `ip_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_ipv6-only.txt` | Only IPv6 link-local addresses, no IPv4 | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `ip_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_no-address.txt` | Interface present but no assigned IP addresses | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `ip_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_empty.txt` | Empty JSON array `[]` — not expected on a real machine but a valid parser input | placeholder | — | — | — |

These six scenarios mirror the parser test plan in
[docs/NETWORK_ADAPTER.md § Fixtures Strategy](../../../../docs/NETWORK_ADAPTER.md#fixtures-strategy).
